chapters.summary: This chapter is about continuity. The product does not just record isolated tasks; it keeps a local operating rhythm where an operator, an assigned harness, and a review harness move work forward through briefs, handoffs, session closeout, and usage records.

attentionItems.title: This may be only the local tool, not the whole workflow
attentionItems.whatHappens: The evidence supports a bounded local product, but it does not prove that this repository covers every surrounding process the owner uses.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: If later evidence shows external companion steps, the manual must keep the boundary explicit instead of implying that operator is the entire operating system for the business.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When product identity behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Init does not repair a broken ledger tree
attentionItems.whatHappens: First-run setup creates the standard .operator/ structure, but an already existing .operator/ stops bootstrap from rebuilding missing pieces.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: A partial or damaged ledger will not be fixed by rerunning bootstrap, so the owner should treat setup as creation, not repair.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When product identity behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Some writes depend on the current task already being set
attentionItems.whatHappens: Several task-bound commands use the current task when no task is supplied and fail closed when there is no active task.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: This is convenient when the workflow is already anchored, but it can surprise an operator who expects every write to stand alone without repository state.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When product identity behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: A claim is not trusted when it is created
attentionItems.whatHappens: A new claim starts unverified, with no verifier and no evidence refs.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: The owner should not read claim creation as correctness; it is only a tracked assertion that still needs support and verification.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When product identity behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Claim status checks are not blanket checks
attentionItems.whatHappens: The verifier rule only runs on claim-backed evidence updates that are recording status. Bare evidence updates do not go through the same trust gate.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: If the owner assumes every status write is equally protected, they can overestimate how much the ledger is actually enforcing.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When ledger lifecycle behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: A late quarantine can overwrite terminal task state
attentionItems.whatHappens: When a claim-backed evidence update is marked quarantined, it always writes quarantined back onto the parent task, even if the task was already complete or verified.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: Closeout is not one-way here. A later quarantine can downgrade a task the owner thought was already finished.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When ledger lifecycle behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Usage import can match more than one shape of source
attentionItems.whatHappens: Usage import can match by exact session ID, by substring in the source reference, by time window, by session overlap, or by scored fallback, and it can hydrate an existing open placeholder instead of always appending a new row.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: Accounting can merge into an existing record instead of creating a fresh one, so a casual import assumption can hide a rewrite.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When ledger lifecycle behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Bootstrap is not a repair path
attentionItems.whatHappens: Running initialization again does not repair a partially initialized ledger tree, and bootstrap does not carry the same executor provenance as normal operational writes.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: A broken local setup may look initialized enough to proceed while still missing parts the workflow depends on.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When ledger lifecycle behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Direct usage intake bypasses session provenance
attentionItems.whatHappens: Manual usage entry writes straight into the dated usage ledger and does not carry the session-import trail that imported usage records have.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: The owner should treat direct usage intake as a first-class accounting write, not as imported history with the same provenance shape.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When ledger lifecycle behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Status-bearing evidence can bypass the verifier gate when no claim is present
attentionItems.whatHappens: A status write on evidence does not automatically go through the claim-based verifier check; that trust rule only applies when a claim is supplied.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: An owner could mistake status for universal verification, but this path is narrower than it looks and can let unsupported trust state into the ledger.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When surfaces and records behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Quarantine can overwrite a task that was already terminal
attentionItems.whatHappens: A quarantine write is not blocked by prior terminal state the way verified promotion is, so late quarantine can downgrade a task.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: Closeout is not one-way; an owner who treats verified or complete as final may miss a later write that changes the task state again.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When surfaces and records behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Usage import is more permissive than exact matching
attentionItems.whatHappens: Usage import can match by more than exact session ID and can hydrate an existing open placeholder instead of always creating a fresh row.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: A caller who expects a strict one-session, one-row import may end up merging or selecting more than intended.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When surfaces and records behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Direct usage intake bypasses the session-import trail
attentionItems.whatHappens: Usage added directly is a first-class ledger write, but it does not carry the same source-session provenance as imported usage.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: Accounting can look complete while the provenance path the owner expected is missing, which complicates review and reconciliation.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When surfaces and records behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Verifier gate is claim-bound
attentionItems.whatHappens: Bare evidence attaches with status can skip verification logic when no claim is supplied.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: If the owner assumes every status change enforces verification, unsupported evidence can look trusted.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When trust and verification behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Identity mode changes the guarantee
attentionItems.whatHappens: Enforced mode fail-closes on unknown or mismatched identities; single-user mode accepts the write and warns.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: The same workflow carries different trust strength depending on configuration, so a trust promise is not universal.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When trust and verification behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Evidence copy is best-effort
attentionItems.whatHappens: The ledger can record the verification change even if the local file copy fails and the file never lands on disk.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: The owner cannot assume the artifact exists just because the ledger says the claim was updated.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When trust and verification behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Quarantine can downgrade a finished task
attentionItems.whatHappens: A later quarantine write can overwrite verified or complete task status.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: Closeout is not one-way, so a late integrity finding can reopen a supposedly settled task.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When trust and verification behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Bootstrap is not self-healing
attentionItems.whatHappens: Init makes the standard layout once, then stops; it does not repair a partial tree and it does not stamp executor identity.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: A broken initial setup can persist unnoticed, and trust audits should not assume bootstrap records prove provenance.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When trust and verification behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Usage import can merge instead of append
attentionItems.whatHappens: Import selection can widen beyond exact equality and can rewrite an existing placeholder in place.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: If the owner expects a strict append-only accounting trail, the import path does not behave that way.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When trust and verification behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Import matching can pull in more than one source
attentionItems.whatHappens: Usage import can match by exact session, by a fragment of the source reference, by time window, by overlap, or by a score-based fallback. That is flexible, but it is not a single exact lookup.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: If the operator assumes one exact session is being imported, the ledger can pick a different record and make the usage trail look cleaner or messier than reality.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When usage and sessions behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Manual usage entries do not carry the same source trail
attentionItems.whatHappens: Direct usage entries are recorded straight into the ledger. They are first-class records, but they are not the same thing as imported session usage and do not give the same source trail.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: A later review can mistake a direct entry for imported accounting unless the owner keeps the two habits distinct.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When usage and sessions behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Session closeout can change the final task state
attentionItems.whatHappens: Ending a session closes usage and may return a task to assigned when no open usage remains, unless someone explicitly chooses another final state. It can also force-close an already closed record.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: If closeout conventions are loose, the final state in the ledger may reflect the last closeout action rather than the full work history.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When usage and sessions behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Import fidelity depends on external session logs
attentionItems.whatHappens: Imported usage is only as reliable as the source logs and conventions available to the importer. This chapter could not verify those logs at runtime.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: When the source data is missing or inconsistent, the ledger can only account for what it can see, not the full runtime history.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When usage and sessions behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: A quarantine can still downgrade a finished task
attentionItems.whatHappens: When evidence is attached with a quarantine status, the parent task can be moved to quarantined even if it had already looked verified or complete.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: Closeout is not one-way. If the owner reads terminal status as final, a later quarantine can reverse that conclusion and change what the business thinks is done.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When handoffs and operating rhythm behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Session closeout can diverge from usage cleanup
attentionItems.whatHappens: Session end usually falls back to assigned when the last open usage closes, but an explicit closeout status can override that fallback.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: The task status you see may not match the usage picture unless the owner knows which record was allowed to win. That matters when handoffs are used to judge whether work is still active.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When handoffs and operating rhythm behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Verification is not a blanket rule on every evidence write
attentionItems.whatHappens: Status-based verification checks run only on claim-backed evidence writes. Bare evidence writes with a status do not enter the same guard.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: If the owner assumes every status change is checked the same way, trust can drift without being noticed. This is an authority boundary, not just a convenience difference.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When handoffs and operating rhythm behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: Usage import can match more broadly than an exact session ID
attentionItems.whatHappens: Import selection can use exact match, substring match, a time window, overlap, or a score-based fallback, and it can hydrate an open placeholder in place.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: A rushed import can attach the wrong session or merge into a row that was already open. That affects accountability even when the ledger still looks tidy.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When handoffs and operating rhythm behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

attentionItems.title: The repository may not be the whole operating system
attentionItems.whatHappens: The reviewed evidence proves a local workflow, not the complete broader system that may surround it.
attentionItems.whyItMatters: If the owner treats this chapter as the whole truth, they may miss external coordination steps that are outside the bounded evidence and therefore still unverified.
attentionItems.resurfaceWhen: When handoffs and operating rhythm behavior or related owner decisions change.
attentionItems.requestedAttention: Review this boundary and decide whether the current behavior is intentional.

ownerDecisions.question: Should this manual treat operator as the whole operating environment, or as one local component inside a larger workflow?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: That choice controls how far the manual is allowed to generalize beyond the repository evidence.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related product identity behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: Keep the chapter scoped to the local ledger unless later evidence proves that the surrounding system boundary is part of the product itself.

ownerDecisions.question: Should task-bound writes rely on the current task by default, or should every write require an explicit task selection?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: The current fallback is useful, but it makes the product depend on existing ledger state.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related product identity behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: If predictability matters more than convenience, favor explicit task selection in future operating practice.

ownerDecisions.question: Should bootstrap stay a first-run setup step, or should it also repair an existing .operator/ tree?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: The answer determines whether a partially initialized ledger is an expected maintenance case or a supported recovery path.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related product identity behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: Treat the current behavior as setup-only unless later evidence justifies adding repair semantics.

ownerDecisions.question: Should the manual treat claim-backed evidence status as the only trusted status path?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: That choice determines whether the owner sees the verification step as a hard trust boundary or just one of several ways to update evidence.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related ledger lifecycle behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: If yes, keep the manual strict: claim creation is only an assertion, evidence supports it, and verification is the separate trust step. If no, define exactly what a bare evidence status is supposed to mean and how it becomes trusted.

ownerDecisions.question: Should a late quarantine be allowed to override a task that already looks complete or verified?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This is a closeout policy decision. It controls whether terminal task state is reversible when a later evidence update arrives.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related ledger lifecycle behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: If no, quarantine needs a stricter rule than the current behavior. If yes, the manual should warn that terminal status is not final against later quarantine evidence.

ownerDecisions.question: Should usage import stay permissive and able to hydrate an open placeholder?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This determines whether usage accounting is an append-oriented history or a merge-oriented correction path.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related ledger lifecycle behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: If no, narrow the selection rule and present usage import as a stricter accounting feed. If yes, explain that imports may match by more than one source shape and may rewrite a placeholder in place.

ownerDecisions.question: Should direct usage intake remain separate from session import provenance?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This decides whether manual usage is a distinct accounting path or just another view of imported session history.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related ledger lifecycle behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: If no, normalize direct usage onto the import model. If yes, state plainly that usage-add is a first-class ledger write with no source-session import trail.

ownerDecisions.question: Should task-bound writes keep the implicit current-task fallback, or should the owner require explicit task identifiers on every write?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: Implicit fallback is convenient, but it ties record creation to repository state in a way that can surprise a user who assumes the target task is always explicit.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related surfaces and records behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: If clarity matters more than convenience, treat explicit task selection as the safer operating rule; if speed matters more, keep the fallback but make the current task visible before each write.

ownerDecisions.question: Should any status-bearing evidence write require a claim, or should bare evidence status writes remain allowed?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This is the boundary between a record that merely exists and a record that can advance trust state.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related surfaces and records behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: If trust needs to be obvious, require a claim whenever status is set; if not, document that status without a claim skips the claim-based verifier path.

ownerDecisions.question: Should quarantine be allowed to downgrade a task that is already verified or complete?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This determines whether closeout is final or can be revised by a later evidence write.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related surfaces and records behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: If terminal states should stay final, quarantine needs a stricter guard; if late quarantine is a valid correction, the owner should accept that verification is not the last possible state change.

ownerDecisions.question: Should imported usage remain permissive and placeholder-hydrating, or should it be narrowed to stricter matching and append-only behavior?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This choice affects whether usage records optimize for smooth reconciliation or for tighter provenance and less accidental merging.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related surfaces and records behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: If reconciliation is the priority, the current merge-friendly path may be acceptable; if provenance is the priority, narrow selection and hydration rules.

ownerDecisions.question: Should evidence attaches with status be rejected when no claim is supplied?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This decides whether verification is a universal gate or only a claim-backed one, which changes how much trust the operator can read from the status field.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related trust and verification behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: Choose the trust bar first, then decide whether the command should fail closed or keep the current claim-bound behavior.

ownerDecisions.question: Should single-user mode remain a warning-only trust mode, or should it fail closed when identity rules are configured?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This sets whether identity mismatches are an audit warning or a hard stop, which directly affects how much the owner can rely on the ledger.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related trust and verification behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: Decide whether the product should optimize for frictionless local work or for strict identity enforcement whenever the map is present.

ownerDecisions.question: Should a local evidence copy failure abort the write instead of letting verification continue?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This decides whether trust can advance without a durable artifact on disk, which is a concrete integrity boundary.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related trust and verification behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: If the artifact matters as proof, require the copy to succeed before trust changes; if not, document that the ledger can outlive the file.

ownerDecisions.question: Should quarantine be allowed to overwrite verified or complete task status?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This decides whether terminal status is final or whether a late integrity finding can still downgrade the task.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related trust and verification behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: If terminal state should be final, block the overwrite; if quarantine must win, document that closeout is reversible.

ownerDecisions.question: Should usage import stay permissive on matching and placeholder hydration, or should it require an exact match and append-only writes?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This sets whether usage import is a flexible reconciliation path or a stricter provenance trail.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related trust and verification behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: Choose between convenience and audit strictness, then align the matching rules and the record rewrite behavior with that choice.

ownerDecisions.question: Should manual usage entries stay as a separate direct-write path, or should every usage row be normalized around imported session records?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: The choice sets whether usage is a flexible accounting input or a more uniform audit trail. Mixing both habits without a rule makes later review harder.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related usage and sessions behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: Separate direct entries are useful when the source log is missing, but they split the accounting trail. Normalizing on import improves consistency, while keeping direct entry preserves flexibility.

ownerDecisions.question: Should session import keep its permissive matching and fallback behavior, or should it require one exact source session?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This choice decides whether the owner favors operational tolerance or import precision. A permissive selector reduces friction, but it also raises the chance of pulling the wrong session into the ledger.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related usage and sessions behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: Exact matching reduces accidental cross-session imports. Permissive matching helps when source logs are messy, but it increases the risk of selecting more than the intended source.

ownerDecisions.question: Should closeout keep the automatic fallback to assigned when usage ends, or should every closeout require an explicit final state?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: This choice determines how much final-state ambiguity the owner is willing to tolerate at the end of a session. Automatic fallback is easier to run; explicit final states are easier to audit.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related usage and sessions behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: The automatic fallback reduces friction, but explicit final states make retrospective review easier because the closeout choice is visible each time.

ownerDecisions.question: Should the generated brief remain the canonical handoff text for the next harness?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: The brief is the main continuity artifact. If it stops being the source of truth, the next harness can inherit stale context or miss the current next action.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related handoffs and operating rhythm behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: Keep the brief as the default handoff artifact unless the owner wants a separate, more formal transfer record for special cases.

ownerDecisions.question: Should task-bound writes keep the implicit current-task fallback, or should the manual require explicit task selection in high-risk work?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: Several write paths depend on the active task when no task is passed. That is fast, but it also makes continuity dependent on repository state.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related handoffs and operating rhythm behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: Decide whether convenience or explicitness matters more for your operating rhythm, then standardize that choice in owner guidance.

ownerDecisions.question: Should session end be allowed to override the fallback assignment when usage closes, or should usage closeout and task status stay strictly coupled?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: The workflow can separate usage cleanup from task status. If the owner wants a simpler lifecycle, that flexibility may need to be narrowed.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related handoffs and operating rhythm behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: Choose which record should win when a session closes and the task state would otherwise fall back, then document that as the expected closeout rule.

ownerDecisions.question: Should usage import stay permissive about session matching and placeholder hydration, or should the owner require stricter selection rules?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: Permissive matching is forgiving, but it also increases the chance of pulling in the wrong session or rewriting an open placeholder unexpectedly.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related handoffs and operating rhythm behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: If accuracy matters more than convenience, tighten the import conventions and tell operators to verify the source session before re-importing.

ownerDecisions.question: Should the manual keep describing this as a local ledger workflow unless broader system boundaries are confirmed?
ownerDecisions.whyItMatters: The evidence does not prove the repository is the entire system. Overstating the boundary would make the owner trust a wider operating model than the evidence supports.
ownerDecisions.resurfaceWhen: Before changing the related handoffs and operating rhythm behavior.
ownerDecisions.currentMeaning: Keep the local framing until the owner confirms the surrounding system, then expand only the parts that are separately proven.

sourceTopology.label: blue-az/operator-control-plane repository snapshot
sourceTopology.scope: Application repository snapshot used by the Running a Multi-Harness Workflow knowledge workflow.

sourceTopology.label: Founder/owner context
sourceTopology.scope: Owner-confirmed intent and deferred decisions supplied before generation.

sourceTopology.label: External runtime and integrations
sourceTopology.scope: Provider-side behavior, event ordering, retries, credentials, live data and operational truth outside the reviewed repository snapshot.
sourceTopology.boundaryNote: The repository can show local reactions and integration code, not every external system's runtime truth.

sourceTopology.label: Unreviewed runtime and owner context
sourceTopology.scope: Operational behavior and owner intent outside the reviewed source snapshot and promoted knowledge.
